_metadata.description: Maps Global Trade Alert (GTA) intervention_type codes to the 8-category geopolitical event taxonomy. GTA is the single best source for Trade Policy Actions and a strong secondary source for Sanctions & Financial Restrictions and Regulatory & Sovereignty Shifts. GTA also provides gta_evaluation ('Red' = harmful/discriminatory, 'Green' = liberalizing, 'Amber' = ambiguous) which informs our severity scoring. Validated against: US tariffs on China 2025 (should appear as trade_policy_actions severity 4-5), EU CBAM (carbon border mechanism, regulatory_sovereignty_shifts), Russia import substitution policies.

gta_evaluation_to_severity.Red: Harmful/trade-discriminatory — primary category for our use case; severity 3-5 depending on scope
gta_evaluation_to_severity.Green: Liberalizing — may indicate reversal of prior restriction; lower severity, track as de-escalation

_metadata.mast_classification_note: GTA uses the MAST (Multi-Agency Support Team) classification for non-tariff measures. MAST chapters are referenced where applicable.

tariff_measures.label: Tariff-based measures
tariff_measures.note: Core trade policy measure. Severity 5 if tariff increase > 25 percentage points or on critical sectors (semiconductors, steel, autos, agriculture). Cross-reference affected_sectors for NAICS mapping.
tariff_measures.validation_note: US Section 301 China tariffs (2018-2025), EU retaliatory tariffs should appear here as severity 4-5 Red interventions

import_quotas_and_bans.label: Import quantity restrictions
import_quotas_and_bans.note: Bans and prohibitions are severity 5 (sudden supply shock). Licensing requirements are lower severity but signal future restriction trajectory.

export_controls_and_bans.label: Export restrictions
export_controls_and_bans.routing_logic: If affected_sectors contains semiconductor, electronics, dual-use, or military categories (NAICS 3344, 3812, 3489, 5417), classify as technology_controls. Otherwise classify as trade_policy_actions.
export_controls_and_bans.note: Export bans on critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, rare earths) map to resource_energy_disruptions. Export controls on chips/AI/dual-use map to technology_controls. All other export restrictions map to trade_policy_actions.
export_controls_and_bans.validation_note: China rare earth export restrictions (2023-2025), US chip export controls (Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Oct 2024 rounds) should appear here

subsidies.label: State aid and subsidies
gta_intervention_types: Government loans at below market rates
subsidies.note: Subsidies are trade-distorting (GTA Red) but rarely cause acute business disruption. Include for sector competitiveness analysis. Large industrial policy packages (CHIPS Act, EU Net-Zero Industry Act) warrant severity 4 due to market-distorting scale.
subsidies.validation_note: US CHIPS Act ($52B, 2022), EU Chips Act (2023), China semiconductor fund rounds should appear as severity 4 Red

trade_remedies.label: Anti-dumping, countervailing duties, safeguards
trade_remedies.note: Trade remedies follow a defined legal process (petition → investigation → provisional → definitive measures). Track announcement date separately from implementation date. Severity 4 if sector is critical or duties > 50%.

local_content_and_investment.label: Local content requirements and FDI restrictions
local_content_and_investment.note: FDI screening expansions (CFIUS, FIRMMA, EU FDI Screening Regulation) are severity 3-4 depending on scope. Buy-local requirements in government procurement are typically lower severity unless company is a major government contractor.
local_content_and_investment.validation_note: US CFIUS expansions, EU FDI screening regulation (2020), India's FDI restrictions on Chinese investment should appear here

sanctions_and_financial_measures.label: Sanctions and financial restrictions
sanctions_and_financial_measures.note: GTA captures some financial measures but OFAC/SDN list is the authoritative source for US sanctions. Use GTA for cross-country sanctions coordination and multilateral financial restrictions. Severity 5 for SWIFT exclusion or comprehensive asset freeze.
sanctions_and_financial_measures.validation_note: Russia sanctions package (Feb-Mar 2022) should appear across GTA + OFAC sources

technical_barriers.label: Technical barriers to trade (TBT / SPS)
technical_barriers.note: Most TBT/SPS measures are routine regulatory activity (low geopolitical signal). Include only if: (a) newly imposed and affecting > $1B trade volume, or (b) clearly retaliatory (e.g., China blocking Australian agricultural imports post-COVID criticism). MAST Chapter B (TBT) and Chapter C (SPS).

data_digital_measures.label: Data localization and digital trade measures
data_digital_measures.note: Data localization mandates (Russia, China, India) are severity 4 for tech and finance companies. Restrict cloud, financial data flows, cross-border data transfers. Also classify as technology_controls if targeting foreign tech platforms specifically.

public_procurement.label: Public procurement restrictions
public_procurement.note: Include for companies heavily dependent on government contracts. Severity 3 if measure explicitly excludes foreign companies from large infrastructure/defense programs.

intellectual_property.label: Intellectual property and technology transfer
intellectual_property.note: Forced tech transfer requirements and IP theft risk are severity 4 for high-tech companies. GTA coverage here is partial — supplement with BIS and specialized tech policy tracking.

migration_and_labor.label: Labor mobility restrictions
migration_and_labor.note: Visa restrictions on skilled workers are severity 3 for companies with globally distributed engineering talent. Brexit-related restrictions are the canonical example.

gta_severity_override_rules.condition: intervention_type in ['Export ban', 'Export quota'] AND affected_sectors overlaps ['lithium', 'cobalt', 'rare earth', 'nickel', 'manganese', 'graphite']
intervention_type in ['Export ban', 'Export licensing requirement'] AND affected_sectors overlaps ['semiconductor', 'chip', 'GPU', 'AI hardware', 'dual-use']
gta_severity_override_rules.validation_note: US Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Oct 2024 export control packages; Netherlands ASML EUV restrictions

gta_severity_override_rules.condition: gta_evaluation = 'Red' AND scope = 'bilateral' AND intervention covers > 50% of bilateral trade value

gta_severity_override_rules.rule: Liberalizing measure on previously restricted sector → de-escalation flag
gta_severity_override_rules.condition: gta_evaluation = 'Green' AND prior Red intervention exists for same jurisdiction-sector pair
gta_severity_override_rules.note: Track as resolution event in the event database; useful for scenario modeling
